response.justification: The candidate meets the 15-year experience minimum and shows genuine breadth across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, plus entrepreneurial initiative as a founder. However, the role is a director-level IC focused on AI strategy, and the resume reflects a strong senior/staff hands-on engineer rather than a recognized AI expert or strategic technical leader. There is no advanced AI degree, no demonstrated AI products or platforms at scale (only recent MCP/agent tooling contributions), and no evidence of advising executives, shaping cloud product strategy, or industry-level thought leadership expected at this level.
strengths: 18+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency backend systems
Founder of an open-source geo-data platform, showing entrepreneurial drive and product ownership
Active knowledge sharing through workshops, conference talks, and open-source libraries
concerns: No demonstrated director/principal-level scope: career trajectory is senior/staff hands-on roles, not strategic leadership
Limited AI/ML depth — recent MCP and agent-tooling work is early and narrow relative to 'recognized AI expertise' requirement
No advanced degree in an AI-related field and no track record advising strategic customers or shaping cloud/AI product strategy

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs. director-level IC role
Lack of recognized AI expertise or shipped AI products
Long, broad engineering experience with founder/startup background
